content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Antigravity Template to Solve Idea Validation
content.seo_description: Overcome the inability to validate ideas with the Antigravity project template. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for freelancers.
content.answer_block: Stop wasting weeks on unvalidated ideas. The Antigravity project template provides a battle-tested scaffolding that allows you to deploy a functional MVP in hours. By pairing this with Claude Code, you automate the heavy lifting, ensuring your client projects are technically sound, scalable, and ready for market testing immediately.
content.intro:
You’ve been there: a client has a 'vision,' you spend 40 hours drafting architecture, and the project stalls because the market never actually asked for it. The graveyard of freelancer projects is filled with half-baked MVPs that never saw the light of day because the validation loop was too slow.

It is time to stop building in the dark. The Antigravity project template isn't just code; it's a structural framework designed for rapid iteration. By combining this with the surgical precision of Claude Code, you can transform a vague concept into a live, interactive prototype before your client finishes their first cup of coffee.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Scaffold
steps.description: Set up the core project structure to ensure all subsequent development follows a modular, production-ready pattern.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project scaffold using the Antigravity standard template, including pre-configured environment variables and authentication hooks.

steps.title: Define the Validation Schema
steps.description: Establish the data models necessary to track user interactions, which are the primary KPIs for your validation phase.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a database schema for tracking user signups and feature usage metrics using the Antigravity schema builder.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "generate schema for user_metrics and signup_flows"

steps.title: Configure the Rapid Feedback UI
steps.description: Implement a high-conversion landing page component that includes built-in tracking for user intent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a responsive landing page component with a waitlist signup form that triggers a webhook on submission.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "add waitlist component with form validation"

steps.title: Integrate Event Tracking
steps.description: Ensure every click and scroll is captured, providing your client with concrete data rather than gut feelings.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Inject client-side event listeners into the UI components to track interaction logs in the Antigravity dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run "instrument all buttons with event tracking"

steps.title: Deploy to Staging
steps.description: Push the validated MVP to a live environment so you can collect real-world feedback within minutes.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the deployment pipeline for a staging environment and generate a shareable preview link.

pro_tips: Always use feature flags; they allow you to toggle functionality off if a validation test fails without breaking the entire build.
Inject 'micro-surveys' directly into the UI components using the Antigravity template to capture sentiment while the user is active.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests simultaneously with your features; this ensures your MVP doesn't degrade as you iterate.
Keep your database schema lean; only track the three metrics that prove your client's core hypothesis.
Automate your daily status reports by having Claude Code summarize the git diffs into a client-friendly email format.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, share the link with 50 targeted users to gather the data required to either pivot or scale the project.
content.conversion_block: Stop the cycle of building features that nobody wants. Leverage the power of Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-impact results for your clients, every single time. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
